blocks.content:
JavaScript can work with **integers** (whole numbers) and **decimals** (numbers with a decimal point).

```javascript
let count = 42;        // integer
let price = 19.99;     // decimal
let temperature = -5;  // negative number
```

Unlike strings, numbers are NOT wrapped in quotes.

blocks.title: Working with Numbers
blocks.code:
let x = 10;
let y = 3;

console.log(x + y);   // Addition: 13
console.log(x - y);   // Subtraction: 7
console.log(x * y);   // Multiplication: 30
console.log(x / y);   // Division: 3.333...
console.log(x % y);   // Remainder: 1
console.log(x ** 2);  // Exponent: 100

blocks.content:
## Useful Math Functions

```javascript
Math.round(4.7)      // 5 - round to nearest integer
Math.floor(4.9)      // 4 - round down
Math.ceil(4.1)       // 5 - round up
Math.abs(-10)        // 10 - absolute value
Math.sqrt(16)        // 4 - square root
Math.random()        // 0.xxx - random number between 0 and 1
```

blocks.title: Math Functions

blocks.explanation: Math.sqrt() calculates the square root of a number. The square root of 49 is 7.

questions.question: What is the result of 10 % 3?
questions.explanation: The % operator gives the remainder. 10 divided by 3 is 3 remainder 1.

questions.question: Which expression returns a random number between 0 and 1?
questions.explanation: Math.random() returns a random decimal number from 0 to 1 (not including 1).
